Balancing a manageable caseload allows the firm to dedicate significant time and energy to each case. Strong relationships within Southern California’s legal network, including Pasadena, provide valuable insights into courtroom expectations and necessary processes.

When facing criminal charges, having a team committed to understanding and addressing your concerns can shift the trajectory of a case. Contact Farris Law Firm to take the first steps toward resolving yours.

Types of Criminal Cases Handled in Pasadena, CA

The legal system in Pasadena handles a wide range of criminal charges, and the team at Farris Law Firm provides representation across numerous offenses.

These include:

  • Drug Possession and Sale
  • Driving Under the Influence (DUI/DWI)
  • Theft charges such as shoplifting or robbery
  • Property crimes related to burglary or vandalism
  • Weapons charges
  • Expungement petitions
  • Domestic violence

Farris Pasadena has specific legal procedures influenced by the California Penal Code, and leveraging that knowledge is key to building a strong defense. Our criminal defense lawyers in Pasadena thoroughly examine every detail, including procedural violations, weak evidence, or alternative sentencing options.

What Impacts Criminal Charges in Pasadena?

Criminal cases in Pasadena involve more than just court appearances. Legal outcomes often depend on various factors, from the strength of the evidence to local policies shaping prosecutorial decisions.

Potential effects of criminal accusations include:

  • Freedom Restrictions: Sentencing may involve jail time, probation, or community service, significantly altering routines and future career opportunities.
  • Financial Consequences: Fines, legal fees, and restitution create financial strain and long-term obligations.
  • Employment Risks: Convictions can lead to suspension or revocation of business or professional licenses and affect job prospects.
  • Public Records: Arrests and charges, even when resolved favorably, might remain part of background checks unless expunged.

Understanding what’s at risk underscores the importance of having a reliable legal strategy. Our Pasadena criminal defense lawyers assist clients in identifying paths to reduce the long-term burdens of their cases whenever possible.

How Does the Criminal Process Work in Pasadena?

Defending against criminal allegations involves several key stages, each requiring careful attention.

While no two cases are the same, here’s an overview of how legal proceedings may unfold in the Pasadena justice system:

  • Arrest and Filing Charges: After being arrested, formal charges are presented in court.
  • Arraignment: This is the first opportunity to respond to charges by entering a plea of guilty, not guilty, or no contest.
  • Evidence Gathering and Pre-Trial Motions: Both the defense and prosecution analyze available evidence. Motions to exclude evidence or dismiss certain charges may also occur during this phase.
  • Resolution or Trial: A negotiated outcome, such as a plea bargain, is reached, or the case proceeds to trial for an argument before a judge or jury.
  • Sentencing: If found guilty, penalties are determined based on the charges, mitigating factors, and statutory guidelines.

Addressing each stage proactively provides better opportunities to shape the outcome. Working with a legal team familiar with Pasadena courthouses, judges, and prosecutors adds to the defense’s effectiveness.

Pasadena Criminal Defense Lawyer FAQs

Does Pasadena have its own court for criminal cases?

Pasadena cases are handled at the Pasadena Courthouse, part of the Los Angeles County Superior Court system. Understanding courtroom protocols specific to this location can streamline the legal process.

What laws govern DUI charges in California?

Under California Vehicle Code §23152, operating a vehicle with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08% or higher results in DUI allegations. Penalties include fines, license suspension, and possible incarceration. Our criminal defense attorneys in Pasadena work hard to help mitigate the potential consequences of a DUI.

Is it possible to have a record cleared?

California Penal Code §1203.4 may allow certain convictions to be dismissed through expungement, but determining eligibility requires a case review. Not all records qualify, particularly those involving severe felonies. Our law firm can help you understand how these laws apply to your case.

Do rights violations during an arrest impact the case?

Violations of constitutional rights, such as unlawful searches and seizures, may significantly affect a case’s outcome. Evidence gathered without proper procedure might become inadmissible in court, potentially weakening the prosecution.

Are plea bargains common in Pasadena?

Many cases in Pasadena result in negotiated agreements rather than full trials, offering reduced penalties or altered charges. Still, these outcomes require strong negotiation and case analysis to ensure fairness and justice. Speak to a criminal defense attorney in Pasadena to learn more about your legal options.
